messages.content:
I am trying to learn Japanese. Can you provide English translations of the sentences, then provide the Romaji and English definitions of each word (in order).
Here is an example:

JAPENESE: ダーズリー 氏 は 大声 を 出した

ENGLISH: Mr. Dursley shouted loudly.

1) ダーズリー (Daazurii) - Dursley (a surname)
2) 氏 (Shi) - Mr./Sir
3) は (wa) - Topic marker
4) 大声 (Oogoe) - Loud voice
5) を (o) - Object marker
6) 出した (Dashita) - Shouted

Please follow the above format and provide the ENGLISH translation, and then Romaji and English definitions of each word (in order). Spaces have been added to help break up the words.

JAPANESE: むしろ 、 ハリー は 言葉 の 通じ ない けがらわしい ナメタジ の よう に 無視 さ れ た 。

ENGLISH: Rather, Harry was ignored like a repulsive slug that couldn't communicate.

1) むしろ (Mushiro) - Rather
2) 、 (、) - Comma (punctuation)
3) ハリー (Harii) - Harry (a given name)
4) は (wa) - Topic marker
5) 言葉 (Kotoba) - Words/language
6) の (no) - Possessive marker
7) 通じ (tsuuji) - To communicate
8) ない (nai) - Negative form of the verb
9) けがらわしい (kegarawashii) - Repulsive/disgusting
10) ナメタジ (Nametaji) - Slug
11) の (no) - Possessive marker
12) よう (you) - Like/resembling
13) に (ni) - Directional marker
14) 無視 (mushi) - Ignore
15) さ (sa) - Verb ending indicating a state/action
16) れた (reta) - Past tense of the verb

I'm sorry, but I cannot visit external websites or access specific URLs. However, if you have any questions or need assistance with Japanese translations or learning, I'm here to help!

I am trying to learn Japanese. Can you provide English translations of the sentences, then provide the Romaji and English definitions of each word (in order). Here is an example:

JAPENESE: ダーズリー 氏 は 大声 を 出した

ENGLISH: Mr. Dursley shouted loudly.

ダーズリー (Daazurii) - Dursley (a surname)
氏 (Shi) - Mr./Sir
は (wa) - Topic marker
大声 (Oogoe) - Loud voice
を (o) - Object marker
出した (Dashita) - Shouted
Please follow the above format and provide the ENGLISH translation, and then Romaji and English definitions of each word (in order). Spaces have been added to help break up the words.

JAPANESE: ダーズリー 夫妻 は よく こんな 風 に 、 ハリー の 目 の 前 で 、 本人 を まるで 無視 し て 話 を し た 。

ENGLISH: The Dursleys often spoke completely ignoring Harry right in front of him like this.

ダーズリー (Daazurii) - Dursley (a surname)
夫妻 (Fuufu) - Couple (husband and wife)
は (wa) - Topic marker
よく (Yoku) - Often
こんな (Konna) - Like this
風 (Fu) - Manner/style
に (ni) - Particle indicating the target or direction
ハリー (Harii) - Harry (a given name)
の (no) - Possessive marker
目 (Me) - Eye
の (no) - Possessive marker
前 (Mae) - Front
で (de) - Particle indicating the place where an action takes place
本人 (Honnin) - Person themselves
